text: This Euractiv article reports that the European Union, for the first time, officially accused Russian authorities of carrying out a cyberattack on a satellite network one hour before the invasion of Ukraine. It explains that the EU declaration condemned malicious cyber activities against the KA-SAT network operated by Viasat, which reportedly disrupted services for authorities, businesses and users in Ukraine and several EU member states. The piece highlights comments by the EU’s foreign policy chief on attributing responsibility to the Russian state and notes concerns about the broader cyber dimension of the war.
